channel.title: FX on New Wayland
channel.description: Recent content in FX on New Wayland

item.title: Anatomy of an FX Transaction
item.description: An FX transaction between floating rate currencies is actually two contracts. One is Bank A putting Bank B in credit with Currency A, and the other is Bank B putting Bank A in credit with Currency B. These contracts are assets of the banks, which can then be discounted into the currency the bank operates in. That creates the matching liabilities, which are deposits and which can then be used to pay people in the other currency area.
